model_answer: Photosynthesis is the process by which plants use sunlight to convert carbon dioxide and water into glucose and oxygen.

tagged_concepts.Photosynthesis Definition: Process where plants use sunlight to convert CO2 and water into glucose and oxygen.
tagged_concepts.Inputs: Carbon dioxide and water are required.

model_answer: Sucrose is a common disaccharide carbohydrate composed of glucose and fructose. It is naturally found in many plants, with high concentrations in sugar beets and sugarcane, from which it is extracted.
